ety: Prk. मुत्तउल्लओ; S. मूत्र+उल+कः; see mūt
def: adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Addicted to passing urine; passing urine much or involuntarily; — a piss-a-bed (cf. mutnā).
s.m. Urine (i.q. mūt, q.v.)
subs: (˚tra+at˚), s.m. (lit. 'past the proper time for voiding urine'), A particular kind of retention of urine; — difficulty in passing urine; slow passage of urine
(˚ra+āgh˚), Affection of the urine, urinary disease; suppression of urine, &c.
s.m., and mūtr-praṇālī, s.f. The urinary passage, or canal (i.q. mūtr-mārg)
s.m. Milky urine (depositing a thick white sediment)
or mūtr-kr̤ićh, s.m. Painful discharge of urine in minute quantities, strangury; — urinary affection (in general, as gravel, stone, &c.)
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit मूत्र (mūtra). Doublet of مُوت (mūt).

ety: moṭ, q.v.+rī = Prk. अडिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. A small bundle.
s.f. = muṭrī, q.v. s.f. A small bundle.
n. f. A small bundle (گتَهرَي).

ety: act. part. of اطرب 'to cause to be affected with lively emotion,' iv. of طرب 'to be affected with lively emotion'
def: s.m. A musician, a minstrel; a singer (Pers. pl. mut̤ribān).
more: (part. act. IV. of طرب) s. m. A musician, singer, minstrel. (Pers. plur. مطربان).

ety: mut̤rib+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Music; minstrelsy; singing.

ety: act. part. of اتفّق 'to agree,' &c., viii of وفق 'to find (a thing
def: part. adj. Consenting, agreeing; consentient, agreed; — corresponding, concurrent (testimonies); — conspiring; conspired, confederated; — united; uniform; unanimous; — s.m. Co-operator; accomplice; conspirator; — coitio
more: adj. وفق suited. Agreeing; conspiring; united; unanimous. muttafiq-ur-rāe , adj. Agreeing in opinion. mutafiq-alah, mutafiq-ul-lafz , adj. Unanimous; nem con. muttafiq h . To agree upon; to concur; to be unanimous.

(part. act. VIII. of وفق) adj. 1. Agreeing, consenting, conspiring, according; united. 2. (subst.) An accomplice, cooperator. 3. Coitio. متفق هونا muttafiḳ honā, To agree together, accord, conspire, confederate, concur, cooperate.
subs: adv. With common (or mutual) consent; unanimously
(-par), To agree together (upon), to concur, to be unanimous; to conspire, confederate; to co-operate.

def: n. m. وﮩم suspected. The accused party. muttaham k. , v. a. To accuse ( ilzām lagānā ).

ety: act. part. of اتّحد, viii of وحد 'to be single; to be one'
def: part. adj. United, made one
more: adj. Made one; united. azlā-i-muttahad . United states.
(part. act. VIII. of وحد) Heb. ) adj. United.

ety: act. part. of اتّہم 'to be suspected,' &c., viii of وهم 'to think, imagine, suspect, &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Suspected; accused, arraigned; — one who is suspected; an accused person

ety: pass. part. and noun of place of اتّکا, viii of (and syn. with
def: s.m. Anything to recline on, a couch, sofa; cushion (cf. takya); — a prop; post, pillar.
more: n. m. A post; pillar; prop. (کهم). Sir par mutakkā sā khaṛā ho jātā hai .

n. m. Dim. maṭkī , n. f. A large earthen jar or pot. 1. Bhalo bhayo merī maṭukī ṭūṭī, maiṅ dahī bechan sechhūṭī . Prov. My pot is broke 'tis well, no tyre I'll have to sell. 2. Jo bachchah apnī mā ko satāëgā to Khudā uske peṭ se nau mahīne tak maṭkā bandhvāëgā . Wom. The child who doth his mother grieve a pitcher to his stomach shall for nine months cleave. (Divine vengeance).
(from وکأ) s. m. Any thing to recline on, a cushion, a couch.

ety: act. part. of اتّقي 'to preserve or keep oneself' (from
def: part. adj. Refraining (from), abstinent, abstemious, temperate, sober (syn. parhez-gār); cautious, wary; God-fearing, devout, pious; — s.m. An abstemious man, &c.
more: adj. وقي See پرﮩـيزگار 1. Refraining. 2. Pious; devout.
(part. act. VIII. of وقي) adj. Abstinent, refraining, abstemious, sober, temperate, pious.

ety: act. part. of اتّکأ, viii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. & s.m. Leaning (upon), reclining, recumbent; laid down to sleep; — supported, propped; — one who leans, or reclines, or sits (esp. upon the heels).
more: part. act. (VIII. from وکأ) Reclining, recumbent.

ety: act. part. of اطّلع 'to see, perceive; to know,' &c., viii of طلع 'to appear,' &c.
def: part. adj. Inspecting, regarding, beholding, perceiving, knowing, becoming informed (of), or acquainted (with)
subs: v.t. To acquaint (one, with, -se, or -par), to inform (of)

ety: act. part. of اطّرد 'to flow, proceed, or continue in an uninterrupted course,' &c., viii of طرد 'to drive before, to pursue,' &c.
def: adj. Flowing, or going on, uninterruptedly, or freely; flowing, current; uninterrupted, unimpeded; prosperous; frequent
more: (part. act. VIII. of طرد Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Prosperous (an affair), flowing, current, frequent.
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. adj. Universal; to be constantly observed (a rule, &c.).

ety: act. part. of اتّسع, viii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Large, wide, broad, spacious; dilated; wide-spread, diffused.
more: (part. pass. II. of تسع) adj. Composed of or relating to nine, enneagon.

ety: act. part. of اتّصل, viii of (and syn. with
def: adj. (fem. -kī), Adjacent, &c.
more: adj. وصل joined. Contiguous; adjoining; joined; adjacent.
(part. act. VIII. from وصل) adj. Joined, near, contiguous, adjoining, continual, uninterrupted, successive, continually.

ety: mūṭh, q.v.+Prk. अओ = S. अ+कः
def: s.m. A handful (of anything); — handle (of a plough, &c.); — a bundle
subs: or muṭṭhā banānā (-kā), To make a bundle (of).

def: n. m. S. मुष्टि 1. A handful. Pakṛā chuṭṭā, aur bharā muṭṭhā . Rus. Seize by the hair, a fistful's there. (Payment obtained by force). 2. A bundle. muṭṭhā bāṅdhnā, yā banānā , v. a. To make a bundle.
more: n. m. S. मथित 1. E. Butter-milk. 2. W. Tyre mixed with some water. Dahī kā maṭṭhā kar lo!
adj. 1. Slow; slack; lazy. 2. Dull ( kund , 2).

def: n. f. S. मुष्टि, Lat. manus hand. 1. The fist. Jo deṅ so muṭṭhī meṅ bāṅdh-kar le jāo, takrār na karo! Clench what you 've got in your fist and be off! dont make a row. 2. Grasp; clutch; gripe. Kyā hī muṭṭhī pakṛī hai! Prov. What a grip he has of his wrist! (Said of a wrestler). 3. A hand, equal to four fingers. 4. The fetlock joint. 5. A handful of corn formerly taken from every field and given in charity to the poor. 6. A small varnished stick given to sucking children to be used as the coral with us. muṭṭhī bhar . A handful. Kabhī ghī ghanā, kabhī muṭṭhī bhar chanā . Prov. Sometimes of butter plenty, sometimes a fistful of gram only. muṭṭhī garm k. , v. a. To bribe. muṭṭhī meṅ , 1. In one's fist. 2. Under one's power or thumb. muṭṭhī meṅ denā , v. a. 1. To place in one's hand. 2. To fill one's fist. muṭṭhiyāṅ bharnā , v. a. To shampoo. baṅdhī muṭṭhī , n. f. 1. The closed fist. 2. A bundle of sticks. 3. A compact body.

ety: Prk. मुट्ठिआ; S. मुष्टिका
def: s.f. The fist; hand; grasp (of the hand), clutch, gripe; — a handful; a handful of corn (formerly taken from every field and given as a charity to the poor); — a hand (measure, equal to four fingers); — the wrist; — the fetlock-joint (in a horse); — a small varnished stick given to a sucking child to be used as the coral with us (syn. maṭkī, q.v.)
more: s. f. Fist, grasp (of the hand), clutch, hand, handful. مٿهي روٿي maṭhī roṭī, A kind of bread: see Qanoon-e-islam, app. p. xxxiv.
subs: (-kī), 'To warm the fist or hand' (of), to give a bribe (to), to bribe
In the fist (of), into the hand (of, -kī); in the clutches (of), under the power (of)
'To fill the fists'; to knead the limbs, to shampoo
s.f. The closed fist; — a bundle (of sticks, &c.); a compact body.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀫𑀼𑀝𑁆𑀞𑀺 (muṭṭhi) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit मुष्टि (muṣṭi), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *muštíš (“fist”). Doublet of مُشْت (muśt). Cognate with Punjabi ਮੁੱਠ (muṭṭha), Bengali মুঠা (muṭha) and Persian مشت (mošt).

def: s.f. corr. of muṭṭhī, q.v. s.f. The fist; hand; grasp (of the hand), clutch, gripe; — a handful; a handful of corn (formerly taken from every field and given as a charity to the poor); — a hand (measure, equal to four fingers); — the wrist; — the fetlock-joint (in a horse); — a small varnished stick given to a sucking child to be used as the coral with us (syn. maṭkī, q.v.)
subs: (-kī), 'To warm the fist or hand' (of), to give a bribe (to), to bribe
In the fist (of), into the hand (of, -kī); in the clutches (of), under the power (of)
'To fill the fists'; to knead the limbs, to shampoo
s.f. The closed fist; — a bundle (of sticks, &c.); a compact body.
wety: Borrowed from Punjabi مِٹّی (miṭṭī) / ਮਿੱਟੀ (miṭṭī), from Sanskrit मृत्तिका (mṛttikā). Doublet of ماٹی (māṭī).

def: adj. Married for a specified time.

n. m. from عـﮩد commanding. Pl. متعاﮩدين 1. A contractor. 2. One who enters into an engagement for the revenue. mutahid , adj. Covenanted. afsarān-i-mutahid . Convenanted officers. shāhān-i-mutahid . Contemporary kings. gair-mutahid . G. G. Uncovenanted.

n. m. اخذه taking. 1. Calling to account; impeachment. 2. Accountability ( javāb-dihī ). 3. Amends; damages. muākhze-dār , adj. Responsible; answerable. mavākhize se barī k. , v. a. To discharge from liability; relieve from responsibility. muvākhize se chhuṛānā , v. a. To redeem in law. muvākhizah k. , v. a. 1. To call to account; impeach. 2. To demand as a due; to claim.
more: (inf. III. of أخذ Heb. ) s. m. Taking satisfaction, retaliating, chastising, calling to a severe account, reprehension.

ety: for A. موافقة, inf. n. of وافق 'to be conformable (to
def: s.f. Conformity, agreement, accordance, accord; concordance, concord; consent, or universal consent, unanimity; correspondence; similarity; fitness; analogy; affinity; congeniality; sympathy, friendliness, friendship; propitiousness
more: n. f. See اتفاق, 1—7. 1. Concordance; conformity. 2. Correspondence; fitness. 3. Propitiousness. 4. Affinity; friendship. muāfaqat rakhnā , v. n. 1. To fit; suit; become. 2. (— khānā ) To agree with (a person). muāfaqat k. , v. n. 1. To reconcile; conciliate anew; to enter into friendship. 2. To comply with.

(inf. III. of وفق) s. f. Conformity, analogy, agreement, accordance, accord, concord, affinity, concordance, propitiousness. موافقت کرنا muwāfaḳat karnā, To enter into friendship, to conform, to close in with, &c.
subs: (-se, or -ke sāth), To entertain friendly feeling (for); to be at accord (with); to agree (with); to suit, fit, become
(-ke sāth), To conform (to), comply (with), close in (with); to enter into friendship (with); — to bring about reconciliation, to reconcile, to conciliate anew

ety: pass. part. of وفّق 'to cause to happen prosperously; to prosper; to favour,' ii of وفق 'to find (a thing, &c.
def: part. adj. Prospered; favoured; assisted.

ety: act. part. of وافق, iii of وفق; see next
def: adj. Conformable, consonant, congruous, agreeing, according, concordant, suiting, suitable; apt, expedient; like, similar, analogous; prosperous, favourable, propitious; — postpn. Conformably, or agreeably (to, -ke), &c.
more: adj. وفق was appropriate. See ṭhīk , 4—7, 16, and jog . 1. Like; conformable; consonant. 2. Suitable. 3. Favorable; propitious. muāfiq , adv. Agreeably to. mūāfiq ānā , v. n. See rās ānā. muāfiq sarishta . Conformably to procedure or practice. muāfiq sir kā , adj. 1. Fitting; appropriate. 2. Middling; not much. 3. Passable; adequate; neither more nor less ( bīch kī rās ). muāfiq h. , v. n. To consist with; to be agreeable to ( ṭhīk ānā , 1).
(part. act. III. of وفق) adj. Conformable, consonant, congruous, apt, like, analogous, fit, agreeing, according, concordant, suiting, favourable, propitious.
subs: v.n, To agree (with, -ko), to suit; to prove favourable, or propitious (to)
adj. (f. -kī), Fitting, appropriate; adequate, sufficient; — middling; not much; passable
(-ke), To be in agreement, &c. (with), to agree (with); to consist (with); to be agreeable (to); to be like or similar (to), &c.

ety: for A. مواهبة, inf. n. of واهب 'to contend (with another
def: s.f. Contending (with another) in liberality and munificence.
more: (inf. III. of وهب Heb. and Aram. ) s. f. To contend with another in liberality and munificence.

ety: for A. مواجہة muwājahat, inf. n. of واجه 'to meet face to face,' &c., iii of وجه 'to turn the face (to
def: s.m. Meeting face to face, coming before, confronting; opposition (of two planets, &c.); presence, appearance; comparing, comparison, collation
more: (inf. III. of وجه) s. m. Presence, confronting, comparison, collation.

ety: pass. part. of وجّه 'to turn, or incline (a thing, or the mind, &c.
def: part. adj. Agreeable, acceptable; approved; — well-founded in reason; reasonable; valid; — of great authority; conspicuous for excellence.
more: adj. وجه turning the face to. Reasonable; valid. Vajah mavajjah .
(part. pass. II. of وجه) adj. Agreeable, acceptable.

ety: pass. part. of وقر 'to honour, revere,' &c., ii of وقر 'to be sedate, or grave,' &c.
def: part. adj. Honoured, revered, respected; venerable, respectable.
more: (part. pass. II. of وقر Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Honoured, revered, respected, respectable.

ety: pass. part. of وقّت 'to appoint a time for (anything
def: part. adj. Fixed or restricted to a certain definite time; — appointed (hour).
more: (part. pass. II. of وقت) adj. Fixed or restricted to a certain definite time.

ety: act. part. of وکّل 'to appoint a lieutenant, or deputy,' &c., ii of وکل 'to entrust (one's affairs
def: s.m. One who appoints a wakīl, i.e. a lieutenant, or deputy, or substitute, or factor, or agent; one who delegates power (to another); a constituent; a client
more: n. m. وکل entrusted. 1. ( Law ) A client. 2. A guardian; trustee.
(part. pass. II. of وکل Heb. and Chald. ) s. m. A guardian, a superintendent, vicegerent. ¶ muwakkil (part. act.) s. m. A constituent, one who delegates power, a client.
subs: vulg. muʼakkal (pass. part. of the same verb), part. adj. & s.m. To whom power is delegated, or a trust is committed; — appointed guardian, or superintendent, &c.; — one to whom power is delegated, or to whom a charge or trust is committed; — a vicegerent; deputy, substitute; superintendent; — a guardian, a trustee; — keeper (of a prisoner), guard, jailor.

ety: for A. موالاة, inf. n. of والي 'to be a friend, &c.; to continue, or follow, without intermission,' iii of ولي 'to be near,' &c.
def: s.f. Pursuing a business steadily or without intermission; — friendship, love, affection; mutual amity; favour, patronage.
more: (inf. III. of ولي) s. f. Friendship, affection, pursuing a business steadily or without intermission.

ety: for A. موالٍ muwāliṉ, act. part. of والي, iii of ولي; see muwālāt
def: s.m. Aider, assistant, defender.

ety: pass. part. of ولّه 'to render sad, or distracted,' ii of وله 'to be sad, or distracted,' &c. (from love or grief
def: part. adj. Rendered sad, &c.; sad, distracted, troubled (with love, grief, or fear).
more: (part. pass. II. or IV. of وله Heb. ) adj. Distracted, troubled (with love, grief, or fear).

def: adj. 1. Written. 2. Dated; bearing date; under date.

ety: for A. مؤاساة muʼāsāt, inf. n. of آسی 'to share (one's property, &c.
def: s.m. Doing good (to), benefiting; benevolence, favour; good, or fortunate, action; — ease, rest; — society, fellowship; — condoling with; reviving by consolation, encouragement, sympathy.
more: (contract. of مواسات inf. III. of أسي Heb. ) s. 1. Society, fellowship. 2. Good or fortunate action. 3. Condoling with.

ety: for A. مواصلة, inf. n. of واصل 'to be constantly with (another
def: s.f. Coming together; doing (anything) incessantly without interruption; — conjunction; — adhesion; connexion; copulation; enjoyment.
more: (inf. III. of وصل) s. f. Conjunction, coming together, adhesion, interview, connexion, copulation.

ety: pass. part. of وشّح 'to put a girdle (upon a lady
def: part. adj. Clad with a girdle, or a scarf, or a baldric (worn pendent from one shoulder across the breast, and under the opposite arm); — ornamented; — (verses) arranged so that the initial letters being put together form some word, or verse; — s.m. An acrostic.

ety: pass. part. of وصّل 'to join, to connect or unite closely,' &c., ii of وصل 'to join; to be united,' &c.
def: part. adj. Happily arrived; arrived; — joined, attached; — reached or attained to; acquired, attained.
part. = موا mūʼā, perf. part. of mūnā, q.v. part. adj. (f. -ī), Dead, lifeless; impotent; dull; base, vile; — s.m. Impotent fellow; wretched creature, wretch
subs: s.m. A washer of dead bodies.

ety: pass. part. of يسّر 'to render easy,' &c., ii of يسر 'to be easy,' &c.
def: part. adj. Rendered easy, facilitated; easy, feasible, practicable; favourable; — ready, prepared; — obtained, attained; attainable, obtainable, procurable
more: adj. يسر being easy. 1. Obtained; attained. 2. Obtainable; procurable. muyassar h., yā ānā ; H. juṛnā , v. n. To be obtained, or obtainable. nā-mayassarī , n. f. Poverty.
(part. pass. II. of يسر Heb. and ) adj. Attained, attainable, procurable, obtainable, facilitated, easy, favourable.
subs: (-ko), To be attained, or attainable (by); to come (to), to be within the reach (of)
s.f. The state of not being able to obtain anything, poverty, indigence.

ety: pass. part. of ضاعف, iii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Doubled; multiplied, increased.
more: (part. pass. III. of ضعف) adj. Doubled, increased, multiplied.

ety: pass. part. of اذاب 'to render fluid, to melt, dissolve,' iv of ذاب (for ذوب
def: part. Melted, dissolved, liquefied; liquid
more: (part. pass. IV. of ذوب Heb. ) adj. Liquid, melted. لعل مذاب laﻌl-i-muẕāb, A liquid ruby, i. e. red wine.
subs: s.m. A liquid ruby, i.e red wine.

ety: pass. and act. part. of the quadr. v. ذبذب 'to cause to dangle, or to move to and fro'
def: part. adj. Dangling; suspended; fluctuating; wavering, vacillating, hesitating, irresolute; uncertain, doubtful; ambiguous
more: adj. 1. Fluctuating. 2. Wavering; hesitating. 3. Doubtful; uncertain ( gol , 3).
(part. act. or pass. of quadril. ذبذب) adj. Doubtful, wavering, hesitating, fluctuating, suspended, ambiguous. (subst.) Doubt, suspense.

def: n. n. ضيق Consequence; importance. Kuchh muzāëqa nahīṅ . It is of no consequence; never mind.

ety: pass. part. of اضاف 'to make to lean or rest (against or upon
def: part. Adjoined, annexed; related; added; increased; — s.m. One who is added to others (esp. to those with whom he has no natural connexion); — (in Gram.) a noun placed in regimine or construction, the governing noun
more: adj. ضيف 1. Added; increased. 2. Annexed. muzāf , n. m. ( Gram .) The possessive case.

(part. pass. IV. of ضيف) adj. 1. Annexed, added. 2. Related. 3. (subst.) A noun placed in construction. 4. One who is joined to others with whom he has no natural connexion. مضاف اليه muẓāf-ilaihi, The noun governed. Plur. m. مضافات muẓāfāt, Things annexed, appendage.
subs: The governed noun in the construct state.

ety: pass. part. of the quadr. v. زعفر 'to dye with saffron'
def: part. Dyed or tinged with saffron; — s.m. A kind of sweet dish, a saffroned pulāʼo.
more: s. m. A kind of dish, a saffroned pulāʼo.

def: (pl. of muẓāfat, fem. of muẓāf), s.f. pl. Things annexed; annexations, additions, appendages
more: n. f. 1. Additions; annexations; appendages. 2. Environs; suburbs. Muzāfāt-i-shahr .

ety: pass. part. of ظفّر 'to cause to be successful, or victorious,' ii of ظفر 'to be successful,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Rendered victorious; victorious; successful, fortunate; superior; — august; — conqueror
more: (part. pass. II. of ظفر) adj. Victorious, conqueror.
subs: adj. & s.m. Rendered victorious and aided (by Heaven); — conqueror, triumpher.

def: ( Gram .) The thing possessed.

ety: inf. n. of مزح 'to jest' (with
def: s.m. A jest, joke; fun.
more: (from مزح) s. m. A jest, joke. ¶ mizāḥ (inf. III.) Jesting (with another person).

ety: for A. مزاحمة, inf. of زاحم, iii of (and syn. with
def: s.f. Hindering; preventing from taking effect; disturbing; — hindrance, prevention, impediment, let, obstruction, molestation; opposition; interruption
more: n. f. Opposition; bar; interruption (آرَ 6, 7, 8). bilā-muzāhimat . Undisturbed.
(inf. III. of زحم) s. f. Prevention, hinderance, impediment.
subs: (-kī), To hinder, prevent, &c.; to disturb; molest, interrupt; oppose
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مزاحمت (muzāhamat), from Arabic مُزاحَمَتْ (muzāḥamat).

ety: pass. part. of ذهّب 'to gild,' ii of ذهب; but prob. formed fr. ẕahab, 'gold'
def: part. Gilded, gilt, washed or done over with gold; worked with gold thread.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَذْهَب (mazhab), from Arabic مَذْهَب (maḏhab).

ety: act. part. of iii of زحم; see muzāḥamat
def: part. adj. & s.m. Obstructing, hindering, preventing, molesting; preventive, obstructive; — inconvenient; troublesome; — an obstructer, a preventer or hinderer; an obstruction, obstacle, preventive, impediment; — muzāḥim honā (-kā), To obstruct, offer obstruction (to), hinder, oppose, resist, molest; to be troublesome.
more: adj. زحم straitened him. Hindering; obstructive. muzāhim h., muzāhimat k. , v. a. To obstruct; offer obstruction; oppose; resist; bar; hinder ( roknā , 2, 3, 5).
part. act. (III. of زحم) Obstructing, hindering. s. m. An obstacle, a preventive, a preventer. adj. Inconvenient.

ety: pass. part. of زيّن, ii of (and syn. with
def: part. Adorned, decorated, embellished; dressed.
more: (part. pass. II. of زين) adj. Adorned, embellished.

ety: for A. مذاکرة muẕākarat, inf. n. of ذاکر 'to call to mind with; to confer with,' iii of ذکر 'to remember,' &c.

Qatl-al-mūzī qabl-al-īzā! Prov. Slay the serpent before he bites you! 2. (Illit. mūjī, mūṅjī ) A miser. Mile tum, saīyāṅ, mūṅjī, joṛte har dam pūṅjī, Kar tāle meṅ band, hāth meṅ rakhte kūṅjī . Play. Kyoṅ be mūzī! nahīṅ māntā? mūzī-panā , n. m. 1. Noxiousness; hurtfulness. 2. Stinginess; parsimony. mūzī kā chuṅgal , The clutches of a tyrant. mūzī kā māl , A miser's property. more: No; not; a negative prefix to nouns or participles, Eng. in, un, dis, non , etc. or the affix less. nā-ittifāqī ; H. an-ban , n. f. Variance; discord ( anban , 1—3). nā-āshnā ; H. an-jān , adj. To do injustice. nā-bālig/?/ ; H. yānā , adj. A minor; ward; youth. nā-bāligī ; H. yān-pan , n. f. Nonage; minority. nā-bakār , adj. See burā , (7, 8). 1. Useless; worthless; vile; good-for-nothing. 2. Wicked; bad. Nā-bakār harkat. nā-balad ; H. gaṅvār , adj. A clown; lout. nā-būd ; H. nashṭ , adj. Non-existent. nā-būd k. , v. a. To anni…

1. A suffix denoting the infinitive mood and (Rus.) the future tense. 2. An emphatic particle. Āo nā! Come, will you! (Why dont you come?)

No, not, a negative prefix to nouns or participles, having the same meaning as the English ones, in, un, dis, non, &c. or the affix less. (In dakh. used like نه na, with verbs). نا آشنا nā-āshnā, adj. Unacquainted; stranger. نا آشناي nā-āshnā-ī, s. f. Friendlessness, the being without acquaintance. نا اميد nā-umed, or nā-ummed, Hopeless, despairing. نا اميدانه nā-ummed-āna, adv. In despair, hopelessly. نا اميدي nā-ummed-ī, s. f. Hopelessness, despair. نا انصاف nā-inṣāf, adj. Unjust. نا اهل nā-ahl, adj. Unworthy, unfit, incapable. نا بالغ nā-bālig̠ẖ, A child not arrived at the age of maturity. نا بخرد nā-ba-ḵẖirad, adj. Unwise. نا بکار nā-ba-kār, Useless, worthless, vile, good for nothing. نا بکاري nā-ba-kār-ī, s. f. Uselessness. نا بلد nā-balad, adj. Without a country, a foreigner, of another country. نا بود nā-būd, 1. adj. That never existed, annihilated, vanished. 2. s. Nonexistence. ن…

ety: Prk. नच्चं; S. नृत्यं; cf. nāćnā
def: s.m. Dancing; a dance, ball; an entertainment (among Hindūs) in which dancing-girls perform (vulg. 'a nautch')
more: A dance. Nāch na jāne, āṅgan ṭeṛhā . Prov. She cannot dance a bit, she says, 'the floor's not fit.' nāch dikhānā , v. a. To dance before one. nāch rāg, nāch raṅg , n. m. 1. Dance and song. 2. Revelry; merriment ( nāch-rāg ). nāch-ghar n. m. A dancing room; a ball room; a theatre. nāch nachānā , v. a. 1. To make one dance. Bhūk maṅgāve bhik ko, bhūk hī sīs nivāë, Bhūk kahāve chorṭā, bhūk hī nāch nachāë . Rus. 'Tis hunger makes you beg, and hunger makes you bow your head, 'Tis hunger dubs you thief, and hunger makes you dance for bread. 2. To worry or harrass one; to lead one a pretty dance ( hairān k .). Yeh qavāīd nahīṅ hai, roṭī nāch nachā rahī hai . This is not drill, it is the belly's dance. ghoṛoṅ kā nāch , An exhibition of artificial horses. lauṅḍoṅ kā nāch , n. m. An exhibition of dancing boys ( bhagat , 4).
s. m. Dance. ناچ نچانا nāch nachānā, To make to dance; to tease.
subs: or nāć-raṅg, s.m. Dance and song; a dancing entertainment; revelry, merriment
s.m. Dancing-room, ball-room; a play-house, theatre
(-ko), To make (one) dance; — to lead (one) a dance, to tease, worry, or harass (one)
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀡𑀘𑁆𑀘 (ṇacca), from Sanskrit नृत्य (nṛtya), which was originally नृत्त (nṛttá) (also see नृत्यति (nṛtyati, “to dance”)). Compare Hindi नृत्य (nŕtya). Cognate with Punjabi ਨੱਚ (nacca) / نَچّ (nacc), Gujarati નાચ (nāc), Sindhi नचु (nacu) / نَچُ (nacu), Bengali নাচ (nac), Chakma 𑄚𑄌𑄴 (nāc), Odia ନାଚ (nāca), Assamese নাচ (nas).

def: v.t. To cause to dance to make (one) dance, to set a-dancing; to spin (a top, &c.)

def: s.m. vulg. corr. of nakshatra, q.v. s.m. A month computed by the moon's passage through the twenty-seven mansions, or a month of thirty days of sixty ghaṛīs each.
more: n. m. S. नक्षत्र Pālī, nakkhattam . A lunar mansion or constellation in the moon's path; an asterism. Besides the common division of the Zodiac into twelve signs, the Hindus divide it into twenty-seven Nachhatras , two and a quarter of which are included in each sign. 1. Tape nakhat mirgsīrā jo tab barkhā pūran jag ho . A hot tenth luner mausion, good rains on all the earth. 2. Jāne kis nichhattar meṅ paidā huā hai? (Said of a wicked boy). Who knows in what lunar mansion this imp was born? nichhat'tarī , adj. Born under a lucky planet; fortunate.

s. m. 1. A lunar mansion or constellation in the moonʼs path, a star or asterism. (Besides the common division of the zodiack into twelve signs, the Hindūs divide it into twenty-seven Nachh̤atras, each of which has its appropriate name, and two and a quarter of which are included in each sign).

def: adj. & s.m. = nakshatrī, q.v. s.m. A person born under a lucky star; a fortunate person (i.q. nakhatrī, q.v.).
more: adj. Born under a lucky planet, fortunate.

ety: nāć˚ = Prk. नच्च(इ) = S. नृत्य (ति), rt. नृत्

Par ghar nācheṅ tīn jane, Kāëth, baid, dalāl . Prov. At other's houses dance these three—Kaith, medicine-man, and touter constantly. 2. To frisk about.

Sounds, and speech, and learning, arts, much wit too you may show, Saith Kabir, you 'll find Him never, few this truth e'er know. 2. Nād, Bed bistār na bāṅchā, vār pār nahīṅ kyūṅ- hīṅ, Abran, baran kahet haiṅ gyānī, voh to jyūṅ kā tyūṅhīṅ . Kabīr. Nor speech nor Beds His breadth hath spanned, nor that end found nor this, Clothed and unclothed the wise Him paint, He is but what he is. nād-biddyā , n. m. The art of singing. nād k . v. n. To roar (as a tiger).

more: s. m. A spout, a canal.

ety: for عليًا نادِ 'call on, or invoke, ʻAlī'
def: s.f. A stone brought by pilgrims from Mecca on which are inscribed benedictions or prayers from the Qorʼān, beginning with the words nādi-ʻalī (such stones are hung as charms round the necks of children).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَلِی (alī), from Arabic عَلِيّ (ʕaliyy).

def: s.f. corr. of the above, q.v.
more: (perhaps, for ناد علي q. v.) s. (dakh.) A stone having generally a verse or certain sentences of the ḳurʼān engraved on it, and worn as a charm round the necks of children. ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

ety: for A. ندامة, inf. n. of ندم 'to repent'
def: s.f. Repentance, penitence, contrition, regret; shame (syn. pashemānī).
more: (from ندم) s. f. Repentance, penitence, contrition, regret.

def: = H ندانا नदाना nadānā, corr. of nā-dān, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = P نادار nā-dār, adj. & s.m. See s.v. نا nā.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نادان (nādān).

def: = H ندان नदान nadān, corr. of nā-dān, q.v.
s. f. Ignorance, folly, foolishness, stupidity, simplicity.
adj. & s.m. = P نادان nā-dān, adj. & s.m. See s.v. نا nā.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نادار (nādār).

ety: na, 'not'+dārad, 'it has,' 3rd pers. sing. of aor. of dāshtan, rt. dār
def: adj. Blank; wanting; gone, lost, extinct; consumed, &c.

ety: intens. n. fr. ندف 'to separate, or to card, cotton'
def: s.m. A cotton-dresser, carder.
more: n. m. A earder ( kaṅḍerā ). Naddāf kī voh laṛkī baiṭhī dukāṅ ke ūpar , Gāloṅ ko sāf kar-ke beche thi khūb-rūī . Double entendre
(adj. superl. of ندف Heb. ) s. m. A cotton-dresser.

ety: fr. naddāf
def: s.f. Cotton-dressing, cotton-carding; — the trade or business of a cotton-dresser.
more: s. f. Dressing of cotton, the trade of a naddāf.

ety: fr. nadī
def: adj. (f. -ī), Coming from, or belonging to, a river; river-born; fluvial; aquatic; — s.m. An aquatic.
s.f. A species of water-reed, Calamus fasciculatus; — the plant Pranna herbacea; — Sesbania œgyptiaca; — the China rose.
s.f. The plant Premna herbacea.

ety: nādh˚ = S. नद्ध, p.p.p. of rt. नह्
def: v.t. To yoke; — to begin, to enter upon (also nāṅdhnā; nāṅdnā).
more: v. a. S. नद्ध bound. To yoke.

def: adj. (f. -inī), Sounding, resonant; sounding aloud.

ety: Prk. नदिआ(?); S. नदिका, rt. नद्; — S. also नदी
def: s.f. A river (the common personification of rivers being fem.)
more: n. f. S. नद् to roar. Pr. naī . A river. 1. ' Pratham pratham Brāhmane,' 'nadī nālābibarjite .' The first place is the Brahman's, bar streams and rivers. (The answer made by a Brahman who was asked to lead in a ford in virtue of the precedence he claimed). 2. Jo purubā puruā ke pāve, sukhlī nadiyā nāū chalāve . Bhoj. Prov. If from the Eastern constellation East winds blow In rivers dry of sailing boats there 'll be a show. 3. Dhīre baho, nadyā, saiyāṅ utreṅge pār! Wom. song. Flow gently, stream, my husband is to go across!
subs: s.m. The tree Terminalia (or Pentaptera) arjun
vulg. nadīj, adj. & s.m. (f. -jā), River-born, water-born, water-produced, aquatic; — any creature born in a river or in river-water; — the tree Terminalia arjun; — the marshy date-tree
vulg. nadīs (nadī+īśa), s.m. 'Lord of rivers,' the ocean

s.m. 'Lover of rivers,' the ocean; — the small tree Vitex negundo; — the tree Baringtonia acutangula; — nadī-kāntā, s.f. 'The beloved of rivers,' the rose-apple, Eugenia jambolana; — the shrub Leea hirta
s.m. (lit. 'fond of river-banks') A species of reed, Calamus rotang.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit नदी (nadī).

def: (for ناد عليّاً) Proclaim Aﻌlī or invoke Aﻌlī.
s.m. corr. of nandī-bail, q.v. (cf. nāṅdiyā).
part. adj. & s.m. Unseen; — who has not (or never) seen; — greedy, famine-stricken, voracious; — eager, longing, wistful; — a hungry or greedy person, a glutton
more: adj. lit . unseen. 1. Greedy; famine-stricken. 2. Longing; wistful. Yeh dīde na-dīde haiṅ dīdār ke . Prov. The eyes are never tired of seeing. nadīdah , n. m. 1. A greedy person. 2. A glutton; gormandizer. nadīdoṅ kī tarah taknā , v. n. To cast greedy eyes on; to stare.
subs: To look or stare wistfully (at), to cast greedy eyes (on).

def: s.f. An Indian hour, or twenty-four minutes (or one-sixtieth of a sidereal day); — a piece of metal on which the hours are struck, an Indian clock, a gong; — a particular measure of length (equal to half a ḍanḍa).
(act. part. of ندم 'to repent,' &c.)., adj. Repenting; penitent, sorry (for); — bashful; ashamed, abashed.
more: (part. act. of ندم) adj. Bashful, penitent, repenting, abashed, ashamed.

ety: v. adj. fr. ندم 'to be a pot-companion,' &c.
def: s.m. Pot-companion, boon-companion; intimate friend, familiar, confidant; — courtier; privy counsellor; — a king's jester or fool.
more: (from ندم) s. m. A companion, intimate friend; courtier.

ety: act. part. of ندر 'to remain alone,' &c.
def: adj. Singular, rare, uncommon, unusual; wonderful; precious; exquisite; choice (cf. shāẕ-o-nādir)
more: adj. Rare; uncommon. Shāz o nādir. nādir-shāhī, nādir-gardī , n. f. Misrule; maladministration, as of Nadir Shah. nād'rī , n. f. The aee of cards. Har chahār nādrī qabl az aftāb . Card-players. Four aces are greater than the Sun-card.
(part. act. of ندر) adj. Rare, wonderful, exquisite, choice. (Plur. نادرات nādirāt, Rarities). نادر روزگار nādir-i-rozgār, The wonder of the world, unequalled.
subs: Name of a king of Persia (who reigned from A.D. 1736 to A.D. 1747)
adj. Of or relating to Nādir-shāh, or his conquests, or misrule; — s.f. The invasion of India by Nādir-shāh; — misrule as of Nādir-shāh
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نادِر, from Arabic نَادِر (nādir).

ety: for A. نادرة nādirat, fem. of nādir
def: adj. = nādir; — s.m. An uncommon thing, a rarity, curiosity; — an incomparable man, a rara avis.
s.f. pl. (of نادرةَ nādirat; see nādira), Uncommon things, rarities, curiosities.

ety: fr. nādir
def: s.f. Rarity; choiceness; — a rarity; curiosity; — (in cards) the ace.
more: s. f. A rarity, rarity, choiceness.

def: Relating to Nādir Shāh, the invasion of India by that prince.
s.m. = نانديا nāṅdiyā, q.v. s.m. The bull and vehicle of Śiva
more: n. m. S. नन्दि The bull and vehicle of Siva.
subs: s.m. Idem; — a show-bull led about from door to door, and taught to obey commands, and to make certain answers, &c.

ety: Prk. नदिआ(?); S. नदिका, rt. नद्; — S. also नदी
def: s.f. A river (the common personification of rivers being fem.)
subs: s.m. The tree Terminalia (or Pentaptera) arjun
vulg. nadīj, adj. & s.m. (f. -jā), River-born, water-born, water-produced, aquatic; — any creature born in a river or in river-water; — the tree Terminalia arjun; — the marshy date-tree
vulg. nadīs (nadī+īśa), s.m. 'Lord of rivers,' the ocean

s.m. 'Lover of rivers,' the ocean; — the small tree Vitex negundo; — the tree Baringtonia acutangula; — nadī-kāntā, s.f. 'The beloved of rivers,' the rose-apple, Eugenia jambolana; — the shrub Leea hirta
s.m. (lit. 'fond of river-banks') A species of reed, Calamus rotang.

ety: Mg. Prk. नअदीयए; S. नव+द्वीप+कः
def: s.m. Name of a town and district in Bengal.
n. f. A stone inscribed with words from the Qurān and worn round the neck as an amulet.
s. (dakh.) A cockle ( Harris ).
v.t. = nādhnā, nāṅdnā, or nāṅdhnā, qq.v. v.t. To yoke; — to begin, to enter upon (also nāṅdhnā; nāṅdnā).
= H ندنندن नदनंदन nadnandan s.m. corr. of nand-nandan, q.v.
= H ندنند नदनंद nadnand s.m. corr. of nand-nandan, q.v.

ety: or naṅdolā; — nāṅd, q.v.+Prk. उल्लओ = S. उल+कः
def: s.m. (dim. of nāṅd), A small earthen pan or water-vessel.
more: n. m. Dim. of nāṅd . S. ननदा A small carthen pan.
s. m. A large earthen pan.

ety: nāʼe, q.v.+ dim. aff. ća = ak = S. क
def: s.m. A small reed (esp. one used by weavers); — a ḥuqqa-snake (i.q. نيچه naića, q.v.).
(dimin. of ناي) s. m. A small reed, a ḥuḳḳa snake.
s. f. (dakh.) See نايکا nāyikā, sign. 2.

ety: Pehl. nāfak; Zend nabi; S. नाभि
def: s.f. The navel; — nave; middle, or centre (of anything)
more: n. f. S. नभि 1. See nābh . 2. Umbilicus. nāf-kā , adj. Umbilical. nāf ṭalnā, jānā, yā ḍignā , v. n. To be dislocated (the navel). nāf-i-shahar . The centre of a city.
s. f. The navel; the middle.
subs: or nāf jānā, or nāf ḍignā or ḍig-jānā (-kī), The muscles of the navel to be displaced or ruptured (through fifting a heavy weight, or other cause)
adj. (f. -kī), Of or pertaining to the navel, umbilical.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ناف (nāf). Doublet of نابھی (nābhi). Related to English navel.

ety: inf. n. of نفع 'to be useful, or profitable,' &c.
def: s.m. Gain, profit, advantage, emolument, proceeds, interest
more: n. m. Profit (فايده 1, 2). Har nām naphā, sab dund kathā . Prov. In Har's name profit lives, all other sounds but empty sound. nafa uṭhānā , v. n. See fāëdah uṭhānā , 1. nafa o nuqsān . Profit and loss.
s. m. Profit, gain, advantage, interest. نفع کرنا nafﻌ karnā, To profit, to benefit.
subs: (-se), or nafʻ karnā, To reap (or to make) profit or advantage (from, or by), to profit, to benefit
adj. Conferring benefits, beneficent; — yielding profit or advantage, profitable, advantageous; — nafʻa-o-nuqṣān, Profit and loss.

ety: rel. n. fr. nāf, q.v.
def: s.m. A bag or bladder of musk, musk-bag (i.q. nāfa-ě-mushk).
more: s. m. A pod of musk. (Also نافۀ مشک nāfa-e-mushk ).

ety: for A. نفقة nafaqat, fr. nafaq, inf. n. of نفق 'to perish,' &c.
def: s.m. The necessary expenses for living; expenditure, disbursement; — a maintenance.
more: s. m. The necessary expenses for living.

ety: see nafl
def: s.m. Plunder (esp. taken from infldels); spoil, prey.

ety: v.n. fr. نفر 'to flee,' &c.; — orig. 'a crowd or body of men (to which one belongs
def: s.m. A servant; a groom; — one person, an individual; one hand (e.g. tīn nafar, 'three persons, three hands; three')
more: n. m. One hand (a workman). Tin nafar . Three hands. naf'rī , n. f. Daily service or wages.
s. m. 1. A groom, a servant. 2. One person; as, تين نفر tīn nafar, Three persons. ¶ nafr, Fright, terrour.
subs: s.m. Settlement of land-revenue, or taxes, &c., made with each individual cultivator himself, independent of any landholder (i.q. raʻīyat-wārī faiṣala).

ety: from nafar
def: s.f. Service, daily work or business (such as a groom's, or the like); — daily wages; — a giant; a demon.
more: s. f. 1. Service, profession, trade (particularly, of a groom). 2. A giant, demon.

ety: inf. n. of نفس 'to desire; to esteem,' &c.
def: s.m. Breath, respiration; — the voice or sound from the breast; — a moment, an instant (syn. dam)
more: (Heb. ) s. m. 1. The breath, respiration. 2. The voice or sound from the breast. (Plur. انفاس anfās ). نفس باز پسين nafas-i-bāz-pasīn, The last breath. ¶ nafs, s. m. 1. The soul, spirit, essence, substance, self. 2. Concupiscence, sensuality. 3. Sperm. 4. Desire. 5. Gravity, pride. 6. Envy. 7. Vice. 8. Penis. نفس الامر nafsu-l-amr, The essence, soul, or foundation of a thing, reality. نفس اماره يا بـﮩيمي nafs-i-ammāra or -bahīmī, Inordinate appetites, concupiscence. نفس پرست nafs-parast, adj. Sensual; sensualist. نفس سبعي يا لوامه nafs-i-sabʼīﻌ or -lauwāma, Irascibility or promptitude to the vindictive passions. نفس کشي nafs-kush-ī, s. f. Mortification of self, self-denial, temperance, penance. نفس مارنا nafs mārnā, To mortify the sensual appetites or self. نفس مطمينه يا ملکي nafs-i-mut̤maʼinna or -malakī, Benevolence.

ety: for A. نفاسة, inf. n. of نَفُسَ 'to be precious,' &c.
def: s.f. Preciousness, exquisiteness, choiceness; — purity; refinement.

ety: fr. naft̤, q.v.
def: s.m. Naphtha, bitumen, or a place where it is found in plenty; — lamp-oil.
more: (from نفط) s. m. 1. Naphtha, bitumen, or a place where it is found in plenty. 2. Lamp-oil.

ety: inf. n. of نفذ 'to penetrate, pervade,' &c.
def: s.m. Penetrating, pervading, piercing through; penetration; — going forth, being issued; passing, leaving behind; — escape (from an enemy); — adj. Arrived, reached (as a letter); — issued; obeyed (as an order).
more: (from نفذ) s. m. Penetrating, pervading, piercing; escape from an enemy. adj. Obeyed (an order).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic نَفَاذ (nafāḏ).

def: s. m. (dakh.) See نفع nafﻌ.

ety: act. part. of نفع 'to be useful, or beneficial,' &c.
def: adj. Useful; profitable, advantageous; beneficial, salutary, wholesome, good.
more: (part. act. of نفع) adj. Salutary, profitable, advantageous, useful.

ety: inf. n. of نفح 'to breathe or diffuse odour'
def: s.m. Breathing; blowing (the wind); diffusing (odour, &c.).

ety: for A. نافٍ nāfiṉ, act. part. of نفي 'to reject,' &c.
def: part. adj. Rejecting; denying; negative.

ety: for A. نَفْيٌ nafyuṉ, inf. n. of نَفَي 'to prohibit,' &c.
def: s.m. Forbidding, prohibiting; prohibition; — denying, disowning, disavowing; denial; — negation; (in Gram.) the negative imperative; — rejecting; refusing; rejection; refusal; — abandoning; driving out or away, expelling, banishing, exiling; — annihilation, non-existence; — (for A. نَهّي nafīyuṉ, v. adj.), adj. & s.m. Negative; — (in Logic) the negative attribute; — (in Alg.) minus; — what is rejected, or thrown away, &c.; refuse; filth; rubbish; froth; scum; dross
more: n. m. Negation; minus. nafī o isbāt . Negation and affirmation.
s. m. 1. Forbidding, prohibition. 2. Rejecting, refusing. 3. Negation. 4. Refuse, filth, refusal, annihilation, nonexistence.
subs: v.t. To forbid, prohibit; to deny; to negative; to refuse; to reject; to banish, &c.

def: s.m. = P نافلت nāfilat, s.f. (for A. نافلة nāfilat, fem. of nāfil, act. part. of نفل 'to present with an extraordinary gift,' &c.), A voluntary act of religion (the observance of which is not positively enjoined); a work of supererogation; — supererogatory prayers; — religious merit.

s.f. = P نافله nāfila, s.m. (for A. نافلة nāfilat, fem. of nāfil, act. part. of نفل 'to present with an extraordinary gift,' &c.), A voluntary act of religion (the observance of which is not positively enjoined); a work of supererogation; — supererogatory prayers; — religious merit.
s.f. A kind of trumpet (smaller than the karnā); — a clarionet; — a fife
s.f. A kind of trumpet (smaller than the karnā); — a clarionet; — a fife
more: (from نفير) s. f. A kind of trumpet.

ety: v. adj. fr. نَفُسَ 'to be precious,' &c.
def: adj. Precious; choice; exquisite; delicate; pure; refined.
more: adj. نفس breath, essence. Pure; exquisite; choice; refined. Nafīs khānā yā kapṛā .

ety: for A. نافية nāfiyat, fem. of nāfī
def: part. adj. Rejecting; denying; negative.

ety: act. part. of نفذ 'to pass through, to penetrate,' &c.
def: part. adj. Passing through, getting through; penetrating, reaching (to); — having effect, generally received or obeyed (an order, or edict, &c.); issued, passed; in force, valid, operative
more: adj. Issued; passed. nāfiz k. , v. a. To issue; pass (an order).
part. act. (of نفذ) Penetrating, passing, getting through, having effect, obeyed (an order).
subs: v.t. To cause to have effect; to pass, issue, put in force (an order)
(-par), To penetrate or reach (to); to have effect (on); — to be issued, &c.

ety: inf. n. of نفخ 'to blow, puff,' &c.
def: s.m. Blowing (with the mouth), puffing; — sounding, winding (a horn); — inspiring, breathing (into); inflation; — swelling; flatulence; — pride, vainglory.
more: (Heb. ) s. m. 1. Blowing (with the mouth), sounding (a horn, &c.). 2. Inspiring, breathing into, inflation. 3. Swelling.

ety: inf. n. of نفل 'to present with an extraordinary gift,' &c.
def: s.m. A voluntary act of religion, the observance of which is not prescribed; certain prayers, the reading of which is not compulsory; a work of supererogation.
more: n. m. Certain Mah. prayers the reading of which is not compulsory.
s. m. A voluntary act of devotion which may be omitted innocently as not being prescribed, a work of supererogation. ¶ nafal, Plunder (from infidels).

def: (inf. n. of نفر 'to be frightened and run away'), s.f.(?) Running away in fright; — fright, terror.

ety: A. nafar+S. अ+कः
def: s.m. A menial servant; a groom, stable-boy, and the like (a form expressive of contempt).
s.f. (dialec.) = nafrāʼī, q.v. s.f. Service in a menial position (as the of groom, &c.).

ety: nafrā, or nafr+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Service in a menial position (as the of groom, &c.).

ety: fem. of nafr, or of nafrā; and = nafr+S. आनी, or nafrā+nī = S. इनी
def: s.f. A woman servant (in a menial position); — the wife of a groom, &c.
more: s. f. Fem. of نفر nafar, sign. 1. q. v.

ety: for A. نفرة, inf. n. of un. fr. نفر 'to fly (from
def: s.f. Flight; terror; — (in Pers. and Urdū) abomination, detestation, horror, abhorrence, aversion, disgust (syn. ghin)
more: n. f. Disgust ( ghin ). nafrat-aṅgez ; H. ghināonā , Disgusting; loathsome. nafrat k., yā khānā , v. n. To be disgusted. nafrīn' , n. f. Curse; malediction.
s. f. 1. Flight, terrour. 2. Abomination, aversion, abhorrence, disgust.
subs: adj. Exciting aversion, &c.; horrible, detestable, disgusting, loathsome
(-se), To fly (from); — to shrink (from) in disgust; to feel aversion or disgust (for), be disgusted (with)
(-se), To feel disgust (for), be disgusted (by, or with).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نَفْرَت (nafrat), from Arabic نَفْرَة (nafra).

ety: imperat. or rt. of nafrīdan, contrac. of nā+āfarīdan; — nafrīn = nā, 'not'+āfrīn = Zend ā-frīna = S. आ+प्रीण; — or perhaps nafrīdan, in the sense 'to detest,' &c. = A. nafr+P. aff. īdan
def: s.f. Detestation, abhorrence; execration; — imprecation, curse, malediction; opprobrious words; reproaches; — contumely, ridicule; — terror, fright.
more: s. f. 1. Detestation, abhorrence. 2. An imprecation, curse, opprobrious words.

ety: v.n. fr. نفس 'to desire greatly,' &c.
def: s.m. (but f. in A.), Breath (of life), animal life; — soul; spirit, self, person; substance, essence, individual thing itself; a person, an individual; — mind, thought; will, pleasure, desire; — body; flesh; blood; — fact, truth, reality; — text (of a work); — concupiscence, carnal or inordinate desire, sensuality, lust, sexual passion; — sperm; — penis; — pride; grandeur, magnificence, pomp; — envy; vice, fault, blemish
more: n. m. Heb. nafach from fuch to breathe. 1. ( nafas ) The breath. 2. The body; person; self. 3. Essence; fact. 4. The sexual passion; lust. 5. The penis . 6. The text of a work. Nafs-i-kitāb. nafs-ul-amr , n. m. The essential truth or fact. nafs-ul-hāl yā amr , The facts or merits of a case. nafs-ul-matlab , The substance; purport. nafs-i-ammārā yā bahīmī , Sensual appetite; concupiscence. nafs-parast, nafsānī , adj. Sensual; carnal. nafs-parvarī , n. f. Selfishness. nafs-kushī , n. f. Self-mortification; selfdenial; penance. nafs mārnā , v. a. To mortify the body. nafsā nafsī . Every one for himself ( āpā āpī ).
subs: The essence, soul, or foundation of a thing; the essential truth or fact of the matter, the reality
or nafs-ě-bahīmī, 'The soul that commands to the indulgence of pleasures and sensual appetites'; the carnal soul; inordinate appetite, concupiscence
or nafs-parwar, adj. & s.m. Gratifying the appetites, sensual, carnal; — one who gratifies his appetites, a sensualist
adj. & s.m. Selfish; — a selfish man
s.f. The gratification of the appetites; sensuality
s.f. Gratification of the appetites; — selfishness
The essence of the case; the facts or merits of the case
or nafs-ě-lawwāma, Irascibility, or promptitude to the vindictive passions
s.f. Mortification of the fleshly lusts; self-mortification; self-denial; temperance; penance
or nafs mārnā, To restrain the passions and appetites, to overcome the lusts of the flesh; to mortify the flesh, or the sensual appetites
The essence of the matter, the substance, or real purport
or nafs-ě-malakī, The spirit of benevolence; benevolence
He himself; it itself; in propria personâ; personally; essentially; intrinsically; — a priori

ety: rel. n. fr. nafs
def: adj. Carnal, sensual; lewd, lustful; — voluptuous; luxurious.

ety: A. نفسانية, abst. s. fr. nafsānī
def: s.f. Animalism, carnality, sensuality; — luxury; — pomp, stateliness, pride, conceit; — egotism; self-opinionatedness; — passion, anger.
more: n. f. 1. ( nafs-parastī ) Sensuality; animalism. 2. Passion; anger. 3. Self-opinionatedness. 4. Pride; conceit. 5. Egotism.
s. f. 1. Luxury, sensuality, carnality. 2. Pride, pomp, stateliness.

ety: rel. n. fr. nafs
def: adj. Of or relating to the soul or self, &c.; carnal, sensual, animal.
more: adj. Of the soul, animal, carnal, sensual.

ety: Pehl. nift; Zend nap; Gr. νάφθα
def: s.f. Naphtha, bitumen.
wety: From Classical Persian نَفْت (naft), from Middle Persian [script needed] (npt' /⁠naft⁠/, “moist, damp; naphtha”), from Old Persian *naftah (“naphtha”).

def: s.f. The Ar. form of نفت naft, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of نفذ 'to penetrate,' &c.
def: s.m. Penetrating, pervading, piercing through (i.q. nafāz̤).

ety: Prk. नागो; S. नागः
def: s.m. A snake, serpent; the hooded snake, or cobra di capello, Coluber naga (which is regarded as sacred by the Hindūs); — a Nāga, or fabulous serpent-demon so called, having a human face with the tail of a serpent (the race of these beings is said to have sprung from Kadrū, wife of Kaśyapa, — or from Su-rasā, — in order to people Pātāla, one of the regions below the earth); — an elephant; — name of several plants, Mesua roxburghii (a small tree with fragrant blossoms); Rottlera tinctoria (used in dyeing); a species of grass, Cyperus pertenuis; Piper betel (i.q. nāg-bel); — one of the five vital airs of the body (that which is expelled by eructation); — poison; — a symbolical term for the number seven (or eight); — name of a district; and of a mountain; — (S. नागं) tin; lead; — one of the astronomical periods called Karaṇs; — adj. (f. -ī), Formed or consisting of serpents; serpentine; relating to serpents, or the serpent-demons; — elephantine; belonging to or resembling an elephant; produced from an elephant
more: n. m. 1. A snake; Coluber naja , Linn. 2. A demigod in the form of a serpent. nāg bhāshā , n. f. A Prakrit said to be used by the serpentine race who inhabit Pātāl (the lowest Hell). nāg-panchmī, nāg-pūjā , n. f. Hin. Wom. 1. Suakeworship. 2. A festival held on the fifth day of the dark fortnight of the month Bhādon. nāg-phāṅs , n. m. A noose used to seize an enemy in battle, resembling the arms of the Retiarii among the Roman gladiators. nāg-phanī , n. f. A prickly plant resembling the hood of a snake; cactus indicus. nāg-daun , n. m. S. नागदमनी Asparagus; asparagus officinalis . A kind of wood, by touching which it is said that fetters spontaneously fall off. nāg yā kālā nāg khilānā , v. a. lit . to cherish a snake. To put one's life in danger.

s. m. 1. A snake (Coluber naja) and a sort of demigod shaped as a serpent. ناگ بهاشا nāg-bhāsh̤ā, A Prākr̤it language said to be used by the serpentine race who inhabit Pātāl. ناگ پنچمي nāg-panchamī, Name of a Hindū holyday, the 5th of Srāwaṇ, sukl-paksh̤, on which day they worship a snake to procure blessings on their children. ناگ پاس ( नागपाश ) nāg-pās, or ناگ پهانس nāg-phāṅs, s. f. A running noose of a rope, &c. a sort of noose or knot, used in battle to entangle an enemy. ناگ کنيا nāg-kanyā, A race of females said to inhabit Pātāl, or the regions under the earth, and to be of serpentine extraction but very beautiful. ناگ لوک nāg-lok, The region under the earth, the abode of serpents and hydras, Pātāla. (Not being accessible to the sun, it is illuminated by very resplendent jewels). 2. An elephant. 3. Lead. 4. Poison.
subs: (˚ga+an˚), s.f. A graduated pole or post for showing the depth of water in a newly-dug pond; a boring-rod or stick driven into the earth
s.m. (lit. 'liked by elephants'), The holy fig-tree, Ficus religiosa
s.f. Name of one of the Prākrit dialects (the language of the Nāgas or serpent-race who inhabit Pātāla)
s.m. 'Elephant-destroyer,' a species of snake, Amphisbaena

vulg. nāg-pās, or nāg-phās, or nāg-phāṅs, s.m. 'Serpent-noose,' a sort of noose or lasso used to entangle or seize an enemy in battle (resembling the arms of the Retiarii among the Roman gladiators; noose (of a rope)
s.f. Wife of a Nāga, or a female of the serpent-race (cf. nāg-kanyā)
s.m. The tree Rottlera tinctoria; — Mesua roxburghii (i.q. nāg-keśar); — Michelia champaka
s.f. Name of a Hindū festival, the fifth day in the light half of the month Śrāvaṇ (on which day they worship a snake to procure blessings on their children)
s.f. The prickly pear, Cactus indicus (a prickly plant resembling the hood of a snake)
s.m. Elephant's tooth or tusk, ivory
s.f. A species of sunflower, Heliotropium indicum

(S. nāga-damana), s.m. 'Snake-stick'; the plant asparagus (syn. mār-ćob); — a kind of wood, Staphylea emodit, by touching which it is said that fetters spontaneously fall off (sticks of it are supposed to keep off snakes, probably because the spotted bark resembles a snake's skin)
s.m. 'Enemy of the elephant,' an epithet of the lion
s.m. The orange (esp. the Silhet orange), Citrus aurantium
s.f. 'Serpent-virgin,' one of a race of very beautiful females, said to be of serpentine extraction and to inhabit Pātāla or the regions below the earth
(or kālā nāg) khilānā, 'To nourish a snake'; to put (one's) life in danger or jeopardy
vulg. nāg-kesar, s.m. A small tree with fragrant blossoms, Mesua roxburghii or ferrea (i.q. nāgesar)
s.f. The flower of the nāg-kesar

s.m. The world or abode of the serpent demons, one of the regions below the earth (otherwise called Pātāla, the abode of serpents and hydras; not being accessible to the sun, it is supposed to be illuminated by very resplendent jewels)
s.f. Mother of serpent-demons,' an epithet of Su-rasā; — red arsenic
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit नाग (nāga).

ety: Prk. नगो; S. नगः
def: s.m. Anything immovable; a mountain, a rock; a tree; — the stone of a ring, or a stone in which a name, &c. is cut; — a precious stone, gem, jewel; — a symbolical expression for the number seven (because of the seven principal mountains)
subs: (˚ga+āś˚), adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Living in or frequenting mountains; — an animal or a creature that lives in or frequents mountains
s.m. 'Rock-splitter,' or 'tree splitter'; the plant Plectanthrus scutellaroides
adj. & s.m. Mountain-born; mountain; — a mountaineer
s.m. 'Chief of mountains,' the Himālaya range of mountains
vulg. nagaj, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Mountain-born; mountain; — a mountaineer.

def: s.m. corr. of نخ naḵẖ, q.v. s.m. A thread (of yarn, or silk, &c.); silk thread (for flying a kite); — a kind of carpet with long pile; — rank, row, line (of soldiers, and the like); — a little; a few (cf. S. nakha).
more: n. m. 1. A mountain. 2. A precious stone ( nagīnah ). 3. A certain number. Aṅgūṭhī nag chār .
s. m. 1. A mountain. 2. The stone of a ring or a stone on which a name, &c. is cut, a jewel, gem, precious stone.

ety: Prk. नग्गओ; S. नग्नकः
def: s.m. A naked mendicant; a class of Hindū mendicants who go naked and carry arms (they sometimes serve as mercenaries to native princes); an individual of that class; — a barbarous tribe inhabiting the hills along the southern borders of Assam.
more: n. m. S. नाम. 1. A class of Hin. mendicants who go naked and carry arms. 2. Barbarous tribes inhabiting the hills along the southern borders of Assām.
s. m. A cast of Hindū faḳīrs.

ety: perhaps corr. fr. P. nā-gah
def: adj. Vacant, void; blank; nought; absent; unemployed; — s.m. A blank; absence; — intermission; adjournment, putting off, respite
more: adj. Vacant; blank; absent; nought. nāgah , n. m. 1. Absence. Do nāgah huiṅ . 2. Adjournment; intermission; respite. nāgah h., yā k . To be absent. bilānāgah , adv. Without fail; regularly; always.
1. adj. Vacant, void, nought, unemployed. 2. s. m. Adjournment, respite, putting off, intermission. ناغه کرنا nāg̠ẖa karnā, To adjourn, discontinue, intermit.
subs: v.t. To intermit; to adjourn, to discontinue; — to render blank or void (an entry, &c.); to be absent

ety: S. नाग+आव्ह+कं
def: s.m. 'The town called after elephants,' Hastina-pura (the ancient Dehli; — syn. nāgāṅg).
s.m. corr. of نقد naqd, q.v.
s.f. Suddenly, unexpectedly; all at once, unawares, abruptly; unseasonably.
more: adv. Suddenly, unexpectedly, all at once, unawares, abruptly.

def: adv. Suddenly, &c. See ناگاه.
adv. contrac. of nā-gāh, q.v. (and cf. next).

ety: nā-gāh, q.v.+āna = Zend ana = S. अन
def: adj. Sudden, unexpected; — adv. Suddenly, unexpectedly; accidentally; all at once.
adj. Sudden. nāgahāṅ, nā-gahānī, nāgāh , adv. Unexpectedly; accidentally ( achānchak , 3). balāë nāgahānī , n. f. A sudden calamity.
adj. Sudden. adv. Suddenly.
adj. & adv. = nāgahān, q.v.
adv. Suddenly. adj. Sudden, unexpected, accidental.
adj. Sudden, unexpected.

ety: for nāṅgal; Prk. णंगलं; S. लाङ्गलं
def: s.m. A plough; — the hooks of a yoke, to which the ropes round the bullock's necks are fastened, and by which the draught is distributed.
more: n. m. S. लङ्गल The hooks of a yoke, to which the ropes round the bullocks' necks are tied, and by which the draught is distributed.

ety: inf. n. of نغم 'to read or sing' (in a low voice
def: s.m. A soft, sweet voice; — a musical sound or tone; — melody; song; modulation; trill, shake
more: s. m. 1. Melody, song, modulation. 2. A musical note. 3. A sweet voice. نغمهسراٴي nag̠ẖma-sarāʼī, s. f. Singing.
subs: or nag̠ma-sāz, or nag̠ma-sarāʼe, s.m. & f. Singer, warbler; musician
or nag̠ma-sanjī, s.f. Singing; music; melody.

def: s.m. (in Pros.) A tribrach.

ety: Prk. नागिणी; S. नाग+इनी
def: s.f. A female serpent; — a female of the Nāga race; the wife of a Nāga or serpent-demon; — a female elephant; — (poet.) a ringlet
more: n. f. S. नागिनी, 1. A female serpent. ' Jo nāgan ghūṅgaṭ base, gorī, kaise jīve bhartār? 'Are lālā! oh to mere ghar kā gāheṛn pīve lahar ulār . 'If, fair one, 'neath your veil's a snake, how doth your husband do?' 'Snake-charmer, Sir, my spouse the poison draws, then sucks me through.' 2. ( Poet .) A ringlet ( zulf ). 3. ( līlī nāgan , Wom.) A quarrelsome woman ( utār , 3).
subs: s.f. A quarrelsome woman; a termagant.
wety: From ناگ (nāg) + ـاِن (-in).

def: s.f. The heart-pea, Cardiospermum hulicacabum.
s.f. = S ناگانگ नागांग nāgāṅg, s.m. See s.v. nāg.
s.m. = S ناگانچلا नागांचला nāgāṅćalā, s.f. See s.v. nāg.

ety: nagan = S. नग्न+nī or ni = S. इनी
def: s.f. A naked woman (i.q. nagnā, and nagnikā).
s.m. (dialec.) = nāgal, q.v. s.m. A plough; — the hooks of a yoke, to which the ropes round the bullock's necks are fastened, and by which the draught is distributed.
more: n. m. 1. A citizen; townsman. 2. A clever or intelligent person. 3. A tribe of Gujrātī Brahmans.
s. m. 1. Name of a tribe of Gujarāt-ī Brāhmaṇs. 2. ( met. ) A cunning or expert person.
s. (dakh.) A plough ( Harris ): see ناگل nāgal.

def: s.f. = nāgarī, q.v. s.f. (fr. nāgar), The Nāgarī (or Deva-nāgarī, the most common Hindī character of writing); — city dialect (as compared with the dialect of the village); — a clever, or crafty, or intriguing woman; — a species of Euphorbia.
s.m. A city, town
more: n. m. Dim. nagrī, naglā, naglī , n. f. S. नगर Pr. naaro . A town; city. Ek nagar ke doë bādshāh, do mil chhatr dhareṅ, Maiṅ toë pūchhūṅ, he sakhi, kis bidh rāj kareṅ? Riddle. Ans. The breasts. One city and two kings, umbrellas both display, I ask you, dear, how can they both have regal sway? nagar-bāsī , n. m. A citizen; an inhabitant of a town. nagar-nārī , n. f. A woman of the town; a courtesan. nagar yā gāoṅ prabandh , The government of a town. nagar-nāyak , n. m. A chief among bards.
s. m. A city, a town. نگر ناري nagar-nārī, s. f. A courtezan.
subs: (˚ra+adh˚), s.m. (f. -ī), Overseer or superintendent of a town; chief civil officer of a city
s.m. Government of a city or town
s.f. A species of the anu-shṭub metre
s.m. The town of Nagar-koṭ (or Kāngrā) in the Panjāb
s.f. A woman of the town, a harlot, prostitute
s.m. The chief person of a town; — the head banker of a town; — a chief among bards
or nagar-vāsī, adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Inhabiting towns; — dwelling in a city or town; — an inhabitant of a town; a citizen
s.m. (f. -inī), One who dwells in a city
(˚ra+up˚), s.m. Skirts of a town, suburbs.
wety: From Sanskrit नगर (nagara).

def: s.m. (dialec.) corr. of نقاره naqqāra, q.v. s.m. A kettle-drum
subs: v.n. 'The drum to turn over'; (met.) the scales or the tables to be turned
To go round proclaiming by beat of drum; to proclaim, to blazon abroad
or naqqāre-kī ćoṭ, adv. By beat of drum, publicly, openly.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نگار (nigār).

ety: fr. nagara
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Relating or belonging to a city or town; civic; urban; town-born, town-bred; (hence) clever, sharp, knowing, knavish; — a citizen; — a person whose breeding and habits are of the city; — a clever, sharp, or knowing person; a wag, a buck; — name of a tribe of Gujarātī Brāhmaṇs; — an individual of that tribe; — the grass Cyperus
subs: s.m. The best kind of betel-leaf
s.f., or nāgar-mothā (see mothā), s.m., A species of sweet-smelling grass, Cyperus pertenuis.

def: s.m. corr. of نقاره naqāra, q.v.
wety: Ultimately derived from Arabic نَقَّارَة (naqqāra), with the widespread change in Indo-Aryan descendants qq > g suggesting an intermediary. Cognate with Punjabi ਨਗਾਰਾ (nagārā), ਨਗਾੜਾ (nagāṛā), Nepali नगरा (nagarā), Odia ନାଗରା (nāgarā). Doublet of نَقَّارَہ (naqqāra).

def: s.m. See s.v. nagar. s.m. (dialec.) = nāgal, q.v. s.m. A plough; — the hooks of a yoke, to which the ropes round the bullock's necks are fastened, and by which the draught is distributed.
s.m. = nāgar-mothā, q.v.s.v. nāgar.
n. m. The Betel plant; Piper betel. nāgar pān , The best kind of Betel leaf.

s.f. (fr. nāgar), The Nāgarī (or Deva-nāgarī, the most common Hindī character of writing); — city dialect (as compared with the dialect of the village); — a clever, or crafty, or intriguing woman; — a species of Euphorbia.
more: n. f. The Sanskrit alphabet and its modifications in Hindī, Marahṭi, etc.
adj. The most common Hindī character of writing; called, also, Deva-nāgarī.

ety: S. नगर+इका
def: s.f. A small town; a town; a village.
more: 1. s. f. ( नगरी ) A town, a village. 2. adj. ( नागरीय ) Of or belonging to a city.

ety: S. नगरीयः
def: adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to a town or city; city-; — civic; urban; — an inhabitant of a town, a townsman.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī or -ā), Living in or inhabiting a town; produced in a town; town-born, town-bred; — polite; clever; cunning; — a citizen; — a polite person; — a superintendent of police.
ex: ہر ناگرک کو ایک دوسرے کی مدد کرنا چاہئے
Every citizen should help their fellow countrymen.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit नागरिक (nāgarika).

ety: S. नागर+इनी

n. m. S. नागरमुस्ता. . A sweet-smelling grass; Cyperus pertenuis , Wat.
more: s. m. Name of a sweet smelling grass. (Cyperus pertenuis: Roxb. ). (Cyperus juncifolius or rush-leaved cyperus: Qanoon-e-islam, gloss. p. lxxvi).

ety: nāgaur+Prk. अओ = S. अ+कः; and Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: adj. Of or belonging to Nāgaur; — s.m. A large, handsome breed of bullocks from Nāgaur (i.q. nāgaurī bail); — nāgaurī, s.m. A native of Nāgaur
more: adj. Good bullocks (from Nāgaur ). nāgaurī asgaṅd. Physalis flexuosa . (?) nāgaurī goṅd , Good gum. nāgaur'ī, nagaurī, nāgaurī bail , n. f. A large handsome breed of horned cattle.
